mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns are negative in more than 55% of rate-cut instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer spending expectations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises by over 0.5pp, or the signal loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, which overwhelms monetary support and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in industrial production reflect immediate supply-side shocks and factory output cuts, which trigger rapid risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a greater than 1.0% INDPRO drop, or the signal reverses direction during liquidity-driven market rebounds.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls while inflation remains above 2.5%, real rates turn deeply negative, stimulating equity risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate income and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or payroll declines fail to predict negative returns when GDP growth remains positive.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP exceeding 0.5% confirms recessionary conditions, prompting institutional deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by over 0.5%, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for Fed balance sheet expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in industrial production and rising unemployment signals a broad-based economic slowdown that accelerates short-term risk aversion, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to generate negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.
